commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=95340b88d6d055df8790f7e28f5988ff243521d4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR teaches realtime transcription capability resolution to cold-load providers from voice-call streaming config and adds a regression test for an xAI streaming provider when only OpenAI is active.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +72. Total +97 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can return the active OpenAI realtime transcription registry while talk.catalog still reports voice-call streaming activeProvider as xAI. I did not run a live Gateway repro, so this is source-reproducible rather than executed locally.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor real behavior proof and a maintainer/author rewrite of the provider-boundary issue before it can pass normal review; it is not a safe automated repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: Fix voice-call streaming provider resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- collect `voice-call.streaming.provider` and `voice-call.streaming.providers` as requested realtime transcription providers
- cold-load a configured streaming transcription provider when the active runtime registry only has another provider loaded
- add regression coverage for `voice-call.streaming.provider = "xai"` with an active OpenAI-only transcription registry

## Root Cause

`talk.catalog` reports `transcription.activeProvider` from `plugins.entries["voice-call"].config.streaming.provider`, but the realtime transcription provider resolver did not use that config path when deciding which provider plugins were requested.

After a Gateway starts, `talk.catalog` can initially list all realtime transcription providers. Once an Agent/Codex turn activates an OpenAI-only runtime registry, `resolvePluginCapabilityProviders({ key: "realtimeTranscriptionProviders" })` sees an active provider and returns it without cold-loading the configured `xai` provider. The result is an inconsistent catalog such as:

```json
{
  "activeProvider": "xai",
  "providers": ["openai"]
}
```

A later `talk.session.create` with `provider: "xai"` then fails with `Realtime transcription provider "xai" is not configured` even though `voice-call.streaming.provider` is set to `xai`.

## Validation

- `npm test -- --run src/plugins/capability-provider-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Local Reproduction Used

On a Gateway configured with `voice-call.streaming.provider = "xai"`:

1. Restart Gateway.
2. `openclaw gateway call talk.catalog --json` includes `xai`.
3. Run a minimal Agent turn to activate the OpenAI runtime path.
4. `openclaw gateway call talk.catalog --json` returns `activeProvider: "xai"` but only `openai` in `transcription.providers`.

This patch keeps the configured streaming provider in the requested provider set, so the resolver cold-loads xAI even when the active registry already contains OpenAI.
target.author: solavrc
